info.name: crypto-gateway
info.description:
Complete flow coverage for crypto-gateway: admin bootstrap, merchant API (invoices + payouts), public checkout, provider webhook ingest, admin operations (recovery, maintenance, forensics), and internal cron.

## Quick start
1. Import this collection AND `crypto-gateway.local.postman_environment.json` (Environments tab).
2. Select the `crypto-gateway (local)` environment.
3. Fill in `adminKey` in the environment. Most other values are auto-populated by test scripts as you run requests — see 'Manual variables' below for the handful that are not.
4. Run `_Setup` → `1. Health` to confirm connectivity.
5. Run `_Setup` → `2. Create merchant` — its test script saves `merchantApiKey`, `merchantId`, `webhookSecret` into the environment for subsequent merchant API calls.
6. Use the per-chain quick-starts under `Invoices / Quick starts` to create sample invoices on each supported chain.

## Manual variables
No test script fills these — set them in the environment yourself when the corresponding requests are on your path:
- `adminKey` — bearer for every `/admin/*` request (must match `ADMIN_KEY` on the gateway)
- `cronSecret` — bearer for `Internal / Cron tick` (must match `CRON_SECRET`)
- `blockcypherIngestToken` — `?token=` auth for the BlockCypher simulate request (must match `BLOCKCYPHER_INGEST_TOKEN`)
- `moneroTxHash` — 64-hex tx hash consumed by `Admin / Debug (Monero)` inspect / force-ingest
- `recoveredTxHash` — explorer-verified on-chain tx hash consumed by `Admin / Payout recovery / Recover held payout`
- `family` — ships with default `solana`; switch to `tron`/`evm`/`utxo` for the `_Setup` fee-wallet requests as needed

## Chaining
Test scripts write `merchantId`, `merchantApiKey`, `webhookSecret`, `invoiceId`, `invoiceReceiveAddress`, `payoutId`, `poolAddress`/`poolAddressFamily`, `lastConsolidationId`/`lastScheduleId`, `heldPayoutId`, `batchId`, `deadDeliveryId`, `orphanTxId`, and per-chain `alchemySigningKey_<chainId>` / `alchemyWebhookId_<chainId>` into the environment so later requests can consume them without copy-paste.

## Webhook simulation
The `Webhooks (simulate provider)` folder fires synthetic provider payloads at the real ingest routes. The two Alchemy items substitute the registered `alchemyWebhookId_<chainId>` into the body and HMAC-sign the exact final bytes into `X-Alchemy-Signature` using `alchemySigningKey[_<chainId>]` — after `_Setup / 4. Bootstrap Alchemy webhooks`, the ingest route accepts them as if from Alchemy. The BlockCypher UTXO item is not signed at all: it authenticates via the `?token={{blockcypherIngestToken}}` URL secret (fill that variable by hand).

## Admin operations folders
- `Admin / Payout recovery` — held (broadcast-outcome-unknown) payout legs: list the queue, attach an explorer-verified `recoveredTxHash`.
- `Admin / UTXO maintenance` — one-shot, idempotent `address_index` repair for the pre-migration-0006 multi-family UTXO bug.
- `Admin / Debug (Monero)` — view-key forensics: per-output inspect-tx, cron scan-state, force-ingest for late payments.

## Server-side config (not used by Postman — reference only)
These env vars affect the gateway's behavior but aren't exercised by any request in this collection. Documented here so operators testing against a deployed gateway know what knobs exist.

**Price oracle fallback chain.** Invoice quoting walks a chained list of providers. Each link tries itself first; on error/timeout/unmapped symbol it delegates. The terminal `noop` link fails explicitly — a full oracle outage surfaces as an error rather than a silently mis-priced invoice; hardcoded rates are never substituted in production. Default ordering with `ALCHEMY_API_KEY` set: `CoinGecko → Alchemy → CoinCap → Binance → noop (explicit failure)`.
- `PRICE_ADAPTER` — `coingecko` (default), `alchemy` (Alchemy-first), or `static-peg` (skip all live sources and serve hardcoded peg values — explicit opt-in for dev/tests only; static-peg is never part of the default chain)
- `COINGECKO_API_KEY`, `COINGECKO_PLAN=demo|pro`, `COINCAP_API_KEY` — optional, raise the per-provider rate budget
- `DISABLE_COINGECKO=1`, `DISABLE_COINCAP=1`, `DISABLE_BINANCE=1`, `DISABLE_ALCHEMY=1` — drop individual providers from the chain

**Log shipping + alerting.**
- `ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL` — `error`/`fatal` logs are POSTed here as JSON. Sliding-window rate-limited to 30/min, with `droppedSinceLast` reported on the next delivery
- `ALERT_WEBHOOK_AUTH_HEADER` — value for the `Authorization` header (e.g. `Bearer xyz`)

See the repository README for the full env-var table.

item.name: _Setup
item.description: Bootstrap flow. Run top-to-bottom in Collection Runner to get a working merchant + API key + (optionally) Alchemy webhook registration.

request.description:
Creates a merchant and returns the plaintext `apiKey` (saved into env) + plaintext `webhookSecret` (saved into env). Neither is recoverable afterward — Postman stores them in the environment so downstream requests chain automatically.

`paymentToleranceUnderBps` / `paymentToleranceOverBps` set the merchant's default invoice payment tolerance in basis points (1 bp = 0.01 %; capped at 2000). `0 / 0` is strict — every cent must match. Per-invoice overrides on `POST /api/v1/invoices` win when set. Tweak via `PATCH /admin/merchants/:id` later.

`addressCooldownSeconds` parks a just-released pool address for N seconds so late payments can still credit the original invoice. `0` (default) preserves legacy immediate-reuse. Capped at 7 days (604800s).

Optional fields not shown in the example body:
- `webhookSecret` — supply your own 64-hex-char HMAC signing secret instead of letting the gateway mint one. **Only stored (and echoed back) when `webhookUrl` is also set** — without a URL it is silently discarded; set the URL later via PATCH and a fresh secret is minted + returned once at that point.
- `confirmationThresholds` — per-chain confirmation override map, chainId-as-STRING keys → integer 1..10000 (e.g. `{"1": 24, "800": 12}`). Resolution at invoice/payout create: this map > `FINALITY_OVERRIDES` env > gateway default (ETH=12, Polygon=256, BTC=6, ...). Snapshotted at create time — later edits never reshape in-flight invoices/payouts. Values below the gateway default are accepted (merchant assumes the reorg risk) and WARN-logged.
- `confirmationTiers` — per-(chain, token) amount-tiered rules keyed `"chainId:TOKEN"` (e.g. `"801:LTC"`), each 1..20 rules of `{ amount, op, confirmations }` evaluated top-down, first match wins. `amount` + `op` must appear together; a trailing rule with both omitted is the catch-all. Ops: `<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`, `=`, `<>`. No match → falls back to the flat threshold. Same snapshot-at-create discipline.

`webhookUrl` is SSRF-guarded (private/loopback/metadata hosts rejected with `INVALID_WEBHOOK_URL`; plain http only in development/test).

item.name: 2b. Update merchant (name, webhook URL, tolerances, cooldown)

request.description:
Mutates any combination of `name`, `webhookUrl`, `paymentToleranceUnderBps`, `paymentToleranceOverBps`, `addressCooldownSeconds`, `confirmationThresholds`, `confirmationTiers`. All optional; body must contain ≥1 field (empty body → 400 VALIDATION).

- `name`: display label, free-form up to 128 chars.
- `webhookUrl`: outbound receiver. SSRF-guarded (private / loopback / metadata hosts rejected). **First-time set** — if the merchant was created without a URL, this PATCH mints a fresh HMAC secret and returns the plaintext once in `webhookSecret` (same contract as create). **Changing an existing URL** does NOT rotate the secret; the signed-HMAC contract stays stable. **Cannot be cleared** — `null` is rejected; deactivate the merchant if deliveries must stop. To rotate the secret itself, use `2h. Rotate webhook secret`.
- `paymentToleranceUnderBps`: paid USD within `bps` of target closes as `confirmed` instead of `partial`. 50 = 0.5 %.
- `paymentToleranceOverBps`: paid USD within `bps` over target closes as `confirmed` instead of `overpaid`. 100 = 1 %.
- `addressCooldownSeconds`: parks a released pool address for N seconds so late payments still credit the original invoice. 300 = 5 min; 0 disables. Capped at 7 days (604800s).
- `confirmationThresholds`: per-chain confirmation override map, chainId-string keys → 1..10000. **Whole-replace**: pass an object to swap the entire map, or explicit `null` to clear back to gateway defaults — partial-key merges are not supported. Values below the gateway default are accepted and WARN-logged.
- `confirmationTiers`: per-(chain, token) amount-tiered rules keyed `"chainId:TOKEN"` (e.g. `{"801:LTC": [{"amount": "3", "op": "<", "confirmations": 1}, {"confirmations": 12}]}`). Same whole-replace / `null`-to-clear semantics.

Tolerance / cooldown / confirmation changes apply to invoices created / releases that happen **after** this PATCH; already-stamped cooldown deadlines and existing invoice/payout snapshots are unaffected.
Paginated list of merchants, newest first. Never leaks `api_key_hash` or `webhook_secret_ciphertext`. Enable the `active` query param to scope to live (`true`) or deactivated (`false`) rows.
Fetch a single merchant by id. Returns the same shape as the list endpoint (minus pagination wrapper). 404 when unknown.

Issues a fresh plaintext API key. The prior key is rejected on the next request. Use this if the old key has leaked — OR if it was lost at create time (`POST /admin/merchants` only returns the plaintext once). The test script overwrites `merchantApiKey` in the environment so downstream requests automatically chain to the new key.

Webhook URL/secret are intentionally NOT rotated here — changing them mid-flight orphans pending outbox rows signed under the old key.

Sets `active=false`. Inbound requests using the merchant's API key start returning 401 immediately. Idempotent. Does NOT invalidate the stored key hash — reactivation with `2g. Reactivate merchant` restores the same credential. If the key has been compromised, rotate (`2e`) first.
Sets `active=true`. Idempotent.

Issues a fresh plaintext HMAC signing secret. Required when the old secret has leaked (or the merchant lost it — `POST /admin/merchants` only returns the plaintext once). The test script overwrites `webhookSecret` in the environment.

**Coordinated rotation required.** Outbox rows dispatched AFTER this call — including any queued before the rotation — will be signed with the NEW secret. The merchant's verifier must hold the new value when those deliveries arrive, or they'll 401 and land in `webhook_deliveries.status='dead'`. Sequence: announce → rotate here → hand the new plaintext to the merchant → they update their verifier. Any dead rows can be replayed via `/admin/webhook-deliveries/:id/replay` once the merchant confirms.

400 if the merchant has no `webhookUrl` — use `2b. Update merchant` to set the URL first (first-time set mints a fresh secret automatically).

Seeds the address pool. Creates N HD-derived addresses per family (evm/tron/solana), marks them 'available', fires `pool.address.created` events that the Alchemy subscription tracker fans out to per-chain webhook registrations.

Idempotent: re-running tops up families below target size. Families whose chain adapter isn't wired on the gateway are reported as `skipped-no-adapter`.

Subsequent invoice creations allocate from this pool. When the available count drops below the refill threshold, a background refill fires automatically — but the initial seed is explicit so operators see the pool filling up before traffic arrives.

Per-family pool snapshot: available / allocated / quarantined / total + highest address_index. Watch this with an alert on available-count under threshold to catch low-supply before POOL_EXHAUSTED fires on a live invoice-create.

Walks the address_pool and for each row derives the EXPECTED address from the current MASTER_SEED at that row's address_index. Compares to the stored address. Any mismatch means the gateway can't sign for that address — the row was either populated with a different seed or by some external tool.

Run this:
- Before funding a fresh pool with real money.
- After deploying where MASTER_SEED might have changed.
- When a payout reports 'Signer-address mismatch' — identify all broken rows in one call.

Response `status` is 'healthy' or 'mismatches-detected'. Checks the FIRST 500 rows per family (ordered by address_index ascending) on every call — there is no pagination; `unscannedBeyondLimit > 0` only signals that more rows exist beyond the window. Seed-rotation corruption is contiguous, so it surfaces within the sample.

item.name: 3d. Consolidate token balance into one pool address

request.description:
Defragment a token balance scattered across pool addresses into a single target. Each leg becomes a `consolidation_sweep` payout under the sentinel system merchant; legs share a `batchId` (the returned `consolidationId`) and ride the regular executor cron.

Use this when a merchant payout fails with `INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE_ANY_SOURCE` AND the warning `single_source_insufficient_consolidate_required` was emitted in the prior `/payouts/estimate` — i.e. the AGGREGATE token balance is enough but it's split across many addresses. Account-model chains pick one sender per payout, so consolidation has to happen on chain via N internal sweeps.

Optional body fields (body is STRICT — unknown fields are rejected with 400 VALIDATION):
- `minSourceBalanceRaw` (decimal string, token's smallest unit) — static per-source dust floor; sources below it are silently dropped. Omit (or pass any floor ≥ "1") to sweep every non-zero source. Passing "0" additionally selects ZERO-balance addresses, producing zero-amount legs that land in `skipped[]` with a planner error — there's no reason to send it.
- `maxSources` (1..200) — cap on legs planned in this call. Sources are sorted by address before truncation, so a re-run resumes exactly where the previous call left off.

When the `CONSOLIDATION_DUST_GAS_MULTIPLIER` env knob is set, a gas-aware dynamic dust floor also applies and `skipped[]` may contain `BELOW_DYNAMIC_DUST_FLOOR` entries (balance cleared the static floor but isn't worth the sweep gas). Consolidating the NATIVE token leaves a gas buffer (2x quoted gas + minimum reserve) behind on each source and REQUIRES a working gas quote (else 400 INVALID_CHAIN).

Returns 202 with `legs[]` (planned successfully) and `skipped[]` (couldn't be planned, usually NO_GAS_SPONSOR_AVAILABLE — fund the sponsor pool and re-run). `legs` can come back EMPTY with every source in `skipped[]` — still a 202. Each leg needs gas: on EVM that's a pool address with native; on Tron the registered fee wallet acts as TRX top-up sponsor automatically.

After all legs reach `confirmed` (poll `/admin/pool/consolidations/{id}`), the target holds the full aggregate and a normal `POST /payouts` succeeds in one tx.

Response codes:
- 202 — consolidation planned (check `skipped[]` for any sources that couldn't be funded)
- 400 BAD_JSON, VALIDATION, INVALID_CHAIN (also when the gas quote fails on a NATIVE consolidation), INVALID_TOKEN, TARGET_NOT_IN_POOL, NO_SOURCES_WITH_BALANCE

Per-leg status + roll-up summary for a previously-planned consolidation. Poll until `summary.pendingOrInFlight === 0` (and `summary.failed === 0`) before kicking off the actual merchant payout from the target.

The `lastConsolidationId` environment variable is auto-populated by the `3d` request's test script — for ad-hoc lookups, replace it with the id you want to inspect.

Response codes:
- 200 — per-leg status array + summary object
- 404 — no consolidation with this id (typo, or the legs were created before the consolidation_sweep migration)

Configure recurring consolidation for a (chainId, token) pair. The cron job `runAutoConsolidations` (registered in scheduled-jobs) sweeps fragmented pool addresses on schedule using the same `planPoolConsolidation` machinery the manual endpoint uses. A tick defers to the next interval when the previous run's legs are still in flight.

**Critical parameter — `minSourceBalanceRaw`**: per-source dust gate. Any pool address holding LESS than this amount (in the token's smallest unit) is silently skipped at fire time. Set above the per-tx gas cost in token units to avoid burning $0.50 in gas to consolidate $5 of dust. Examples:
- Polygon USDT (~$0.01/tx): `"100000"` (0.1 USDT) is fine
- Ethereum L1 USDT (~$0.50/tx): `"50000000"` (50 USDT) — anything less is gas-negative
- Tron USDT with delegated energy ($0/tx): `"1"` — sweep everything

**`maxFeeUsd`** (optional, USD decimal string e.g. "5.00") — gas-window gating: when set, the cron quotes the live low-tier network fee before firing and DEFERS the run to the next interval while the fee exceeds the ceiling (a FAILED quote also defers — don't sweep blind). Omit for no ceiling; clear later via PATCH `"maxFeeUsd": null`.

**`maxSourcesPerRun`** caps legs per tick (default 25). Bounds CPU + avoids broadcasting many simultaneous consolidations that drive gas prices up. Remaining sources picked up next tick.

**`intervalHours`** 1..720 (max 30 days). After creation, `nextRunDue = now + intervalHours` — the FIRST fire is one full interval out; use `POST /admin/pool/consolidate` for an immediate sweep. Body is strict — unknown fields are rejected.

Response codes:
- 201 — schedule created
- 400 — BAD_JSON, VALIDATION, INVALID_CHAIN, INVALID_TOKEN, TARGET_NOT_IN_POOL
- 409 — SCHEDULE_ALREADY_EXISTS (one per (chainId, token); PATCH the existing one)

query.description: true | false

request.description: List all schedules, newest first. Query filters: `chainId`, `token`, `enabled` (true/false). Returns `{ schedules: [...] }`.

item.name: 3h. Get one auto-consolidation schedule (incl. last-run snapshot)

request.description:
Returns the schedule including the snapshot of the most recent cron firing: `lastRunAt`, `lastConsolidationId`, `lastLegCount`, `lastSkippedCount`. Use the `lastConsolidationId` with `GET /admin/pool/consolidations/{id}` for full per-leg status of the most recent run.

The `lastScheduleId` environment variable is auto-populated by the `3f` request's test script.

Update one or more fields on an existing schedule. All fields optional but at least one required: `targetAddress`, `intervalHours`, `minSourceBalanceRaw`, `maxFeeUsd`, `maxSourcesPerRun`, `enabled`. Unknown fields are rejected (400 VALIDATION). `chainId`/`token` cannot be changed — delete and recreate.

`maxFeeUsd`: pass a USD decimal string (e.g. "5.00") to set/raise/lower the gas ceiling, or explicit `null` to clear it (back to "always run").

Updating `intervalHours` recomputes `nextRunDue` from the existing baseline (`lastRunAt` if the schedule has fired, else `createdAt`) — lower interval = next fire sooner, higher = deferred. Changing `targetAddress` re-validates pool membership.

Response codes:
- 200 — updated schedule
- 400 — BAD_JSON, VALIDATION (empty/unknown-field body), TARGET_NOT_IN_POOL (when targetAddress changes)
- 404 — SCHEDULE_NOT_FOUND

Removes the schedule entirely. Prefer PATCH `enabled: false` when you expect to re-enable later — that preserves the lastRun snapshot for forensic value. DELETE is for 'this schedule is wrong, start over'.

item.name: 3. Inspect HD payout sources (admin balance snapshot)

query.description: Only `pool` exists today; the legacy `fee` value is silently coerced.
Force RPC reconciliation. Slow (15-30s); off by default.

request.description:
There is no separate fee-wallet table any more — every HD-derived address in `address_pool` is a candidate payout source. Use this endpoint to see ledger-derived spendable balances per chain/address (confirmed inbound − confirmed outbound − active reservations). The `selectSource` picker reads the same data when planning payouts, so what you see here is exactly what the picker will see.

For a quick "can this chain fund a payout right now?" check, the per-chain `tokens` rollup is the fastest signal.

Returns one row per family (evm, tron, solana) with its adapter capability + current configuration (or null when unset). Monero and UTXO never appear — Monero is inbound-only in v1 and UTXO has no separate fee-payer concept.

Capability values:
- `none`     — EVM today (no native feePayer separation pre-account-abstraction). A fee wallet can still be registered, but the planner ignores it.
- `top-up`   — Tron today. The fee wallet holds spendable TRX and sponsors gas top-up txs to source pool addresses. Sources still burn TRX for energy/bandwidth, but the operator funds ONE wallet instead of seeding every pool address.
- `delegate` — Tron future (Phase 4). Fee wallet stakes TRX and DelegateResources energy so payouts burn zero TRX. The /tron/freeze + /tron/delegate endpoints already exist to stage it, but the planner does not consume delegations yet, so the adapter deliberately reports `top-up`.
- `co-sign`  — Solana. Fee wallet signs every payout as the tx's feePayer, covering sig fee + ATA rent.

`configured.mode`: `hd-pool` = address is an existing address_pool row (key derived from MASTER_SEED on demand); `imported` = external key uploaded via /import, encrypted at rest.

item.name: 4b. Fee wallet — pick existing HD pool address (POST .../use-pool)

request.description:
Registers an existing HD pool address as the family's fee wallet. Zero new secrets — the address is already derivable from MASTER_SEED at its recorded `address_index`, same path pool payouts use.

The server verifies the address actually exists in `address_pool` for the family before writing. Returns 404 POOL_ADDRESS_NOT_FOUND if not.

Swap by DELETE then POST again. The `{{family}}` URL segment (env variable, default `solana`) must be `evm` | `tron` | `solana` | `utxo` — `monero` is rejected with its own 400. `utxo` is accepted but only useful when the pool was seeded with `families: ["utxo"]`.

item.name: 4c. Fee wallet — import external private key (POST .../import)

request.description:
Use when you have an external wallet (common for Tron operators who don't want to re-stake 14 days' cooldown on a fresh address).

Private key is encrypted at the boundary via secretsCipher (AES-256-GCM — same primitive used for webhook HMAC secrets). Plaintext is only in-memory for the encrypt call, never logged or stored.

Cross-checked at import: the declared `address` must equal the private key's derived address (via ChainAdapter.addressFromPrivateKey). Mismatch → 400 ADDRESS_KEY_MISMATCH, no row persisted. Key parse failure → 400 INVALID_PRIVATE_KEY. Both caught before storage, so you can't silently persist a mismatched key.

Idempotent. Planner immediately falls back to self-pay on next plan.

IMPORTANT (Tron): removing the registration does NOT unfreeze staked TRX or reverse active delegations. For Tron, always call /undelegate on each active delegation and /unfreeze on staked TRX BEFORE removing, or funds stay locked on-chain.

Energy + bandwidth snapshot for the Tron fee wallet (via /wallet/getaccountresource). Operators poll this after freeze/delegate to verify the setup.

`energyAvailable` includes both the wallet's own stake AND resources delegated IN — it's what the chain actually lets the account consume today before TRX burn kicks in. `bandwidthAvailable` includes Tron's ~600/day free allowance.

Wraps /wallet/freezebalancev2. `balance` is in sun — 100_000_000 = 100 TRX. `resource` is ENERGY (for TRC-20 execution) or BANDWIDTH (for tx bytes).

Staked TRX is reclaimable via /unfreeze (14-day cooldown). Not a burn.

Signed with the fee wallet's key via signerStore and broadcast through the same path production payouts use.

item.name: 4g. Tron — delegate resources to a pool address (POST .../tron/delegate)

request.description:
Wraps /wallet/delegateresource. `balance` is STAKE in sun (not energy amount) — the delegated stake produces a network-variable energy-per-day rate, see /tron/resources to tune.

Response includes `receiverIsInPool`: true when the receiver address is an `address_pool` row (typical), false when delegating to an external address (allowed but warned).

Script this across every pool address you want to free from TRX-burn payouts.

Reverses a prior /delegate. Reclaimed stake rejoins the fee wallet's free pool (not immediately withdrawable — it's still staked, just no longer lent out).

Must be called BEFORE /unfreeze on any delegated stake: Tron rejects an unfreeze that would leave active delegations unfunded.
Begins the 14-day unlock period on staked TRX. Funds become withdrawable after that (separate withdrawexpireunfreeze call). Used when rebalancing or decommissioning a fee wallet.

item.name: 0. Discover chains (wired vs registry-only, with token list)

query.description: evm | tron | solana | utxo | monero (anything else → 400 BAD_FAMILY)
true = only adapters loaded into deps.chains; false = registry-only; other values ignored

request.description:
Discovery endpoint for the dashboard's bootstrap picker. Returns every chain in the static `CHAIN_REGISTRY` (minus the synthetic dev chain `999`), plus any wired-but-unregistered chainIds appended at the end, with readiness flags per chain so the dashboard can decide bootstrap state in a single call:

- `wired`             — a `ChainAdapter` for the chainId is loaded in `deps.chains`
- `webhooksSupported` — chain *can* use Alchemy webhooks (EVM + Solana). **False for Tron, Bitcoin/Litecoin and Monero** — RPC-poll only, no webhook concept. Frontend should hide the 'register webhook' CTA when this is false.
- `alchemyConfigured` — operator's `ALCHEMY_CHAINS` env actually includes this chainId.
- `webhooks`          — row exists in `alchemy_webhook_registry`. Only meaningful when `webhooksSupported=true`.
- `feeWallets`        — family-scoped: true when the chain's family has ≥1 address in the shared pool (a candidate payout/gas source) AND the chain is wired. Always false for Monero — it uses its own subaddress allocator, never the shared pool.
- `detection`         — `alchemy` | `rpc-poll`. Derived from `webhooksSupported`.
- `bootstrapReady`    — `wired && (!webhooksSupported || webhooks) && feeWallets`. RPC-poll chains (Tron, UTXO) can be ready with just `wired+feeWallets`.

Also returns the static token list per chain (`contractAddress: null` for natives like ETH/BTC/XMR/SOL) and `confirmationsRequired` — the operator-level threshold (FINALITY_OVERRIDES env, else gateway default; per-merchant overrides are NOT reflected). The dev chain (chainId 999) is excluded — it's a test-harness chain, never bootstrap real Alchemy webhooks against it.

Never returns secrets (signing keys, seeds, env).

query.description: 1..500, default 100
Narrow to one chain id.

item.name: 4b. Manually register a signing key (dashboard-created webhook)

request.description:
Only needed when you created a webhook directly in the Alchemy dashboard UI (skipping bootstrap) OR when a bootstrap run returned `persisted: false` on a `created` row. Encrypts the signingKey via `SECRETS_ENCRYPTION_KEY` and upserts into `alchemy_webhook_registry`. Re-running with the same `chainId` rotates (dashboard delete+recreate workflow).

Most operators should NOT need this — bootstrap persists signing keys automatically.

Idempotently creates one ADDRESS_ACTIVITY webhook per chain on Alchemy, pointing at `$GATEWAY_PUBLIC_URL/webhooks/alchemy`. The webhook URL is NOT accepted in the body — it's built from the `GATEWAY_PUBLIC_URL` env var on the gateway (security: prevents a leaked ADMIN_KEY from redirecting Alchemy's traffic). Safe to re-run: list-first matching (same URL + network) prevents duplicates.

Omit `chainIds` to default to the gateway's `ALCHEMY_CHAINS` env, then the mainnet set `[1, 10, 137, 8453, 42161, 43114, 56]`. Chains Alchemy doesn't serve (Tron, Bitcoin/Litecoin, Monero) come back as `status: "unsupported"`.

Per-chain result statuses: `created` (new webhook — `signingKey`, `persisted`, `placeholderRemoved` returned), `existing` (already there — `webhookId` only; Alchemy never re-reveals signing keys), `unsupported`, `failed` (Alchemy API error in `error`). Note `failed`/`unsupported` rows still return HTTP 200 — always inspect `results`.

Alchemy requires ≥1 watched address at create, so the gateway seeds a placeholder (EVM zero address / inert deterministic Solana pubkey) and removes it immediately post-create (`placeholderRemoved: true`; re-runs self-heal older webhooks too). Pass `seedAddressByChainId` (e.g. `{"1": "0xYourAddress"}`) to seed your own address instead — operator seeds are KEPT watched.

**500 `BOOTSTRAP_PARTIAL_FAILURE`**: webhook(s) created in Alchemy but the encrypted registry write failed. The 500 body STILL contains `results` — the `signingKey` there is your ONLY copy; re-register it via `4b. Manually register a signing key`. The test script harvests keys regardless of status code for exactly this reason.

Per-chain signing keys are persisted encrypted in `alchemy_webhook_registry`. request.description:
Paginated view of individual pool addresses with allocation/disable state, ordered by (family, addressIndex) for stable paging. Filter with `family` and/or `status`; `total` counts the whole filtered set so you can page past `limit`.

Each row is `{ family, address, addressIndex, status (available|allocated|quarantined), disabledAt (epoch ms | null), allocatedToInvoiceId }`. `quarantined` = operator-parked (disabled). Cross-reference `GET /admin/balances?kind=pool` to see which addresses hold funds — empty + `available` rows are the safe disable candidates.

The test script captures the first available, non-disabled row into `poolAddress` / `poolAddressFamily` for the disable/enable requests below.

Response codes:
- 200 — `{ addresses[], total, limit, offset }`
- 400 VALIDATION — bad `family`/`status`/`limit`/`offset` VALUE (unknown query KEYS are ignored on this endpoint)

Park a pool address so the allocator stops handing it out during normal allocation. SOFT preference — under pool exhaustion the allocator borrows parked addresses back rather than failing invoice creation, then re-parks them on release.

Semantics:
- `available` row → parks immediately (`status` becomes `quarantined`).
- `allocated` row → keeps serving its current invoice (response shows `status: "allocated"` with `disabledAt` set), re-parks on release.
- Idempotent: re-disabling preserves the original `disabledAt`.

Always safe: a parked address stays in the pool, is still swept by consolidation, and late payments follow the usual cooldown/orphan path. The address is canonicalized through the family adapter (EVM case-insensitive; Tron/Solana case-sensitive). Body is STRICT — exactly `family` + `address`.

`{{poolAddress}}` / `{{poolAddressFamily}}` are auto-populated by the '3b-1. List pool addresses' test script (first available, non-disabled row). Override for ad-hoc use.

Response codes:
- 200 — updated PoolAddressAdminView under `address`
- 400 — BAD_JSON / VALIDATION (unknown or missing body fields)
- 404 — ADDRESS_NOT_FOUND (not in that family's pool, or family adapter not wired)

Clears the disable intent (`disabledAt` → null) and un-parks an idle row (`quarantined` → `available`). A currently-`allocated` row keeps serving its invoice and simply releases back to `available` as normal now that the flag is gone. Idempotent. Mirrors 3b-2 (same strict two-field body, same address canonicalization).

Response codes:
- 200 — updated PoolAddressAdminView under `address`
- 400 — BAD_JSON / VALIDATION
- 404 — ADDRESS_NOT_FOUND (not in that family's pool, or family adapter not wired)

request.description:
Every pool address with a disable intent (`disabledAt` set), optionally filtered by `family`, ordered by (family, addressIndex). Includes rows currently parked (`status=quarantined`) AND rows the allocator borrowed back under pool-exhaustion pressure (`status=allocated` with `disabledAt` still set — they re-park on release). Not paginated.

Response codes:
- 200 — `{ addresses: PoolAddressAdminView[] }`
- 400 VALIDATION — invalid `family` value

Seeds the DEDICATED Monero subaddress pool — separate from the shared address pool because Monero is inbound-only (the gateway holds the view key, never the spend key; do NOT put `monero` in `POST /admin/pool/initialize`, that fails with 500).

Idempotent top-up: every wired Monero chain (1000 mainnet / 1001 stagenet / 1002 testnet) is brought up to `initialSize` subaddress rows (`already-sufficient` when already there). Returns `{ results: [] }` when no Monero adapter is wired. `initialSize` is 1..200; omit it to fall back to the `MONERO_POOL_INITIAL_SIZE` env, then the built-in 20.

New indices always start above the pool max AND the legacy per-invoice counter high-water mark (and >= 1 — index 0 is the operator's primary address), so pooled subaddresses never collide with previously issued ones. Watch `highestIndex` from 3l against your wallet's subaddress LOOKAHEAD — a lookahead below it makes deposits to the newest subaddresses invisible to the wallet RPC.

The pool self-heals (background refill when available < 3) and released subaddresses observe `MONERO_POOL_COOLDOWN_SECONDS` (default 3600) before reuse, shrinking the late-payment misattribution window (Monero has no payment IDs).

Response codes:
- 200 — `{ results: [{ chainId, outcome: topped-up|already-sufficient, priorCount, added }] }`
- 400 — BAD_JSON / VALIDATION (initialSize out of 1..200)

Per-chain Monero subaddress pool snapshot: `{ stats: [{ chainId, available, allocated, quarantined, total, highestIndex }] }`, sorted by chainId (1000 mainnet / 1001 stagenet / 1002 testnet). Empty array until 3k (or the first self-healing refill) has run.

`highestIndex` is the highest account-0 subaddress index ever derived — keep your wallet's subaddress lookahead above it or deposits to the newest subaddresses become invisible to the wallet RPC. `available` includes rows still in their post-release cooldown (the allocator skips those until it lapses); `quarantined` is reserved and 0 in practice. The pool has no hard cap — a persistently large `total` (soft-warn at 200) means churn is outrunning reuse: raise MONERO_POOL_INITIAL_SIZE or lower MONERO_POOL_COOLDOWN_SECONDS.

item.description: Merchant-scoped invoice API. Requires `merchantApiKey` (auto-populated by `_Setup / 2. Create merchant`).

request.description:
Uses `chainId`, `token`, `amountRaw` from the environment. Flip chains by changing the environment values; or use one of the `Quick starts` requests that pre-set them.

Alternative shape: omit `amountRaw` and provide `fiatAmount` + `fiatCurrency` to let the gateway quote the crypto amount against the price oracle.

**Idempotency.** `externalId` is your dedup key (Stripe-style). A retry of the same `(merchantId, externalId)` returns the original invoice — same body, same `receiveAddress` — instead of allocating a new pool address. Safe to send the same POST twice after a network blip. The default body uses a fresh `runExternalId` per request so Collection Runner loops don't collide; pin `externalId` to a constant value to exercise the idempotent-replay path explicitly.

Optional per-invoice webhook override: pass `webhookUrl` + `webhookSecret` together to route this invoice's events to a URL/secret different from the merchant-account default. Both must be supplied as a pair (one without the other is a 400). The URL is echoed back in the response; the secret is stored encrypted at rest and never returned. Dispatch precedence: invoice override → merchant default → skip. See `Create invoice (with per-invoice webhook)` for an example.

Same as the parametric Create invoice, but sets a per-invoice `webhookUrl` + `webhookSecret`. Events for THIS invoice (`invoice:payment_detected`, `invoice:payment_confirmed`, `invoice:completed`, etc.) dispatch to the per-invoice URL signed with the per-invoice secret instead of the merchant-account default. Response echoes `webhookUrl`; `webhookSecret` is never returned.

Per-invoice payment tolerance override. `paymentToleranceUnderBps: 50` (0.5 %) means a 1.99 USD payment against a 2.00 USD invoice closes as `completed` instead of sitting at `processing` with `extraStatus=partial`. `paymentToleranceOverBps: 100` (1 %) means a 2.02 USD payment closes as `completed` with `extraStatus` null instead of `overpaid`. Each is capped at 2000 bps (20 %); omit either to inherit the merchant default. Snapshotted at create time — a later `PATCH /admin/merchants/:id` does not retroactively reshape this invoice.

request.description:
Paginated list of the authenticated merchant's invoices. Default sort `createdAt DESC`. All filters optional, AND-combined. `merchantId` is always taken from the API key — no way to list another merchant's invoices.

Response shape:

```json
{
  "invoices": [ { ... } ],
  "limit": 25,
  "offset": 0,
  "sortBy": "createdAt",
  "sortDir": "desc",
  "hasMore": false
}
```

**Filter notes:**
- Exact vs Contains: `externalId`/`toAddress` are exact-match; `externalIdContains`/`addressContains` are case-insensitive `LIKE %term%` (LIKE metacharacters `%`/`_` in the term are escaped).
- `txHash` joins the `transactions` table — finds the invoice a customer payment was attributed to. A payout hash won't match here (payout txs aren't ingested as transactions); use `GET /payouts?txHash=` for those.
- USD bounds CAST decimal strings to REAL; legacy token-amount invoices (no `amountUsd`) drop out when an `amountUsd*` bound is set.
- Page via `limit` + `offset`; `hasMore` is computed without a COUNT(*) so the list path stays cheap.

**Validation errors (400):** `BAD_STATUS`, `BAD_CHAIN_ID`, `BAD_TIMESTAMP`, `BAD_NUMBER`, `BAD_BOOLEAN`, plus `VALIDATION` for an unknown `sortBy`/`sortDir` or `*Min > *Max`.

Force-expires the invoice before its `expiresAt` window elapses. Only `pending` and `processing` invoices are eligible — partial-paid (`processing` + `extraStatus=partial`) included.

**NOT idempotent:** re-running against an already-terminal invoice (`completed`, `expired`, `canceled`) returns **409** with `error.code = EXPIRE_NOT_ALLOWED`, not 200. Unknown ids, malformed (non-UUID) ids, and other merchants' invoices all return **404** `NOT_FOUND` — indistinguishable by design.

Side effects on success: publishes `invoice.expired` (merchant webhook fires; a per-invoice `webhookUrl` override is honored) and the invoice's pooled receive addresses return to rotation after the merchant's address cooldown.

Response body is `{ "invoice": { ... } }` with `status: "expired"` — no `amounts` / `transactions` blocks (use `GET /api/v1/invoices/:id` for those).

item.name: Universal USD invoice ($25, all families, any token)

request.description:
USD-pegged invoice payable from ANY supported chain in ANY registered token — including native gas tokens. Difference vs. `Multi-family — USDT`:

- `amountUsd` instead of `amountRaw` — invoice tracks the $25 target across whichever token/chain pays.
- **Universal token acceptance.** Stable + native are equally first-class. Pay with USDC on Arbitrum, ETH on Ethereum, USDT on Tron, TRX on Tron, POL on Polygon, AVAX on Avalanche, BNB on BSC, SOL on Solana — even mix them: $15 USDC + $10 ETH satisfies a $25 invoice. Each transfer's USD value is computed at detection time via the pinned rate snapshot and summed.
- Rates are pinned at creation for 10 minutes (the rate window).
- `chainId` + `token` are the *primary* family (used for the legacy `receiveAddress`). Real acceptance is everything in `acceptedFamilies`.

Response contains `acceptedFamilies`, `receiveAddresses[]`, `amountUsd`, `paidUsd`, `rates`, and `rateWindowExpiresAt`. Follow up with `Get invoice` to see the live USD breakdown + per-tx detail (including any reverted/detected rows).

The checkout page (`GET /checkout/:id`) renders one row per (chain, token) combination via the `payableTokens` field — pre-computed minimum amounts ready to pay.

Alternative invoice-creation shape: instead of `amountRaw`, pass `fiatAmount` + `fiatCurrency`. The gateway quotes the token amount via the live price-oracle chain (CoinGecko-first by default; fails with an explicit error if every oracle is down — the static USD peg is only used under `PRICE_ADAPTER=static-peg`, dev/tests) and persists the quoted rate on the invoice.

item.name: Payouts
item.description: Merchant-scoped payout API. Requires at least one funded HD-derived address (visible via `_Setup / 3. Inspect HD payout sources`). The picker automatically falls back to JIT gas top-up when the source has the token but lacks native — no fee-wallet pre-registration needed.

request.description:
Plans a payout AND reserves its funding source in the same request — the response payout is already `status: "reserved"` (`planned` is a legacy state new rows skip). On account-model chains (EVM/Tron/Solana) `sourceAddress` is set to the HD address the picker chose (richest token-holder first, gas headroom checked); on UTXO chains (BTC/LTC) it stays `null` — coin selection runs across every owned UTXO at broadcast time.

Because selection is synchronous, insufficient-funds errors surface HERE, not as a later webhook: 503 `INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE_ANY_SOURCE` (no single source covers the amount — the message says whether consolidating fragmented balances would fix it), 503 `NO_GAS_SPONSOR_AVAILABLE` (token holder exists but nothing can top up its gas), 400 `MAX_AMOUNT_EXCEEDS_NET_SPENDABLE` (`details` carries `suggestedAmountRaw` / `suggestedAmount` / `suggestedAmountUsd` for a retry). Only the broadcast is async: the executor is kicked in the background right after the POST (fast execution) or on the next cron tick, moving the row `reserved` → (`topping-up` →) `submitted` → `confirmed`. Track via `payout.*` webhooks or `GET /payouts/:id`.

**Token symbol** can be any registered on the chain — stablecoins (USDC/USDT) **and** natives (ETH/POL/BNB/AVAX/TRX/SOL/BTC/LTC). Discover per-chain symbols via `GET /admin/chains`. Monero chains reject with 400 `PAYOUT_NOT_SUPPORTED_ON_FAMILY` (inbound-only in v1).

**Amount input (pick exactly one):**
- `amountRaw` — integer string in token's smallest unit (e.g. `"1000000"` = 1 USDC, `"1000000000000000000"` = 1 ETH)
- `amount` — human decimal string (e.g. `"1.5"`); too many decimal places → 400 `BAD_AMOUNT`
- `amountUSD` — fiat-pegged decimal (e.g. `"10.00"`); resolved via the price oracle at create time and snapshotted to `quotedRate`/`quotedAmountUsd`. Oracle outage → 503 `ORACLE_FAILED`.

**Optional fields:** `feeTier` (low/medium/high — the planner budgets `medium` when omitted), `batchId` (grouping id, honored on single-create too), and a per-payout webhook override: pass `webhookUrl` + `webhookSecret` together (one without the other is a 400). The URL is echoed back; the secret is encrypted at rest and never returned. Unknown body keys are rejected (strict schema, 400 `VALIDATION`).

request.description:
Quotes low/medium/high fee tiers AND previews the HD source the planner would pick — all WITHOUT planning anything or writing a reservation. Same strict body schema as `Plan payout`; pick a tier on the real POST via `feeTier`.

**Response blocks:**
- `tiers` — per-tier `nativeAmountRaw` + best-effort `usdAmount` (null on oracle miss). `tieringSupported: false` on Tron (no priority concept), on Solana with no recent samples, and when the fee quote failed (tiers zeroed + `fee_quote_unavailable` warning — the estimate still 200s).
- `source` — the address the planner would fund from (`null` if none qualifies). On UTXO chains it's a synthetic aggregate: `address: "<utxo-aggregate>"`, `derivationIndex: -1`. On Tron, `feeCoveredByDelegatedEnergy: true` means the real cost for THAT source is ~0 despite the chain-level worst-case `tiers` figure.
- `topUp` — present only when the source lacks native gas; `sponsor: null` predicts the plan would fail `NO_GAS_SPONSOR_AVAILABLE`.
- `feeWallet` — Tron top-up capability diagnostic: live balance vs `topUpRequired` vs `shortfall`, and whether it was `selected` as sponsor.
- `warnings` — e.g. `no_source_address_has_sufficient_token_balance`, `single_source_insufficient_consolidate_required` (aggregate is enough — consolidate), `no_gas_sponsor_available`, `fee_quote_unavailable`, `fee_quote_degraded`, `fee_market_uniform` (three identical tiers isn't a bug), `insufficient_utxo_balance`.

**Tier semantics:** EVM 0.8×/1.0×/1.5× on `estimateFeesPerGas`; Solana 25th/50th/75th percentile of recent prioritization fees; Tron single tier; UTXO sat/vB tiers fed to real coinselect. Native payouts that almost-but-don't fit throw 400 `MAX_AMOUNT_EXCEEDS_NET_SPENDABLE` with `details.suggestedAmount*`. `amountUSD` input + oracle outage → 503 `ORACLE_FAILED`.

Mass-create up to 100 payouts in one call. Per-row errors do NOT abort the batch — partial success is normal. Every planned row carries the same `batchId` (top-level in response) so you can `GET /api/v1/payouts?batchId=<uuid>` to list them.

**Rate limit:** costs N tokens from the merchant's per-minute quota (not 1). A 100-row batch against a 60-per-minute merchant will 429 on the 61st row; cap batches to what your quota can afford.

**HTTP 200** on partial success. Inspect `results[i].status` + `.error` / `.payout` per row. `400` only when the whole array fails schema (oversize, missing `payouts`, etc.).

exec: if (body.batchId) { pm.environment.set('batchId', body.batchId); console.log('saved batchId =', body.batchId); }

item.name: Checkout (public)
item.description: Unauthenticated checkout view — the URL your frontend or Telegram bot renders for the payer.

request.description:
Public, unauthenticated payer view — the shape your checkout page / Telegram bot renders. The invoice id (a high-entropy UUID) is the only credential. Rate limited per client IP (429 + `Retry-After` on abuse).

**Included:** `status` + `extraStatus`, primary `chainId` / `token` / `receiveAddress`, `acceptedFamilies` + `receiveAddresses` (`[{family, address}]` — render one address per family for multi-family invoices), `requiredAmountRaw` / `receivedAmountRaw`, `fiatAmount` / `fiatCurrency`, the USD-path fields (`amountUsd`, `paidUsd`, `overpaidUsd`, `rates`, `rateWindowExpiresAt`), and `createdAt` / `expiresAt` / `confirmedAt`.

**`payableTokens`** (USD-path invoices only; `null` on legacy invoices): one entry per (family, chainId, token) the gateway can settle, each with `amountRawMinimum` (ceil-rounded raw units), `amountDisplay` (human decimal, e.g. "0.04" ETH), `decimals`, `rate` (pinned USD per whole token) and `address`. Render a chooser like "Pay 100.00 USDC on Polygon" without doing any decimals or rate math yourself. Amounts are valid until `rateWindowExpiresAt` (10-minute pinned window).

**Excluded on purpose:** `merchantId`, `externalId`, `metadata`, `webhookUrl`, tolerance/confirmation policy fields, `updatedAt`.

404 `NOT_FOUND` for unknown AND malformed (non-UUID) ids alike — bots can't probe the keyspace.

item.name: Webhooks (simulate provider)
item.description:
Fake provider webhook payloads sent through the real ingest routes — two Alchemy simulators and one BlockCypher simulator, each authenticating exactly the way the real provider does.

**Alchemy items** (EVM + Solana): the pre-request script substitutes the registered `alchemyWebhookId_<chainId>` into the body (the ingest route resolves the HMAC key by the payload's `webhookId` from the DB registry — an unregistered id 404s), then computes `HMAC-SHA256(body, signingKey)` hex into `X-Alchemy-Signature` — same flow the real Alchemy service uses. Both values are saved by `_Setup / 4. Bootstrap Alchemy webhooks`; without bootstrap, register the placeholder webhookId + a signing key of your choosing via `_Setup / 4b (POST /admin/alchemy-webhooks/signing-keys)` and set `alchemySigningKey` manually.

**BlockCypher item** (UTXO/LTC): NOT HMAC-signed — BlockCypher doesn't sign payloads. It authenticates via the `?token={{blockcypherIngestToken}}` URL secret instead (must match `BLOCKCYPHER_INGEST_TOKEN` on the gateway); its pre-request only randomizes the tx hash.

request.description:
Simulates an incoming ERC-20 USDC transfer (1.000000 USDC — `rawValue` 0xF4240 against the mainnet USDC contract) to the receive address saved by the most recent `Create invoice` on Ethereum (chainId 1).

The pre-request script (1) freshens `createdAt` — the route rejects payloads older than 10 minutes with 401 STALE_PAYLOAD, (2) mints a unique event `id` — a repeated id returns `{ received: true, deduped: true }` for 24h without reprocessing, (3) randomizes the tx `hash` so every send is a NEW payment (repeat hashes are idempotent no-ops via the UNIQUE (chain_id, tx_hash, log_index) constraint), (4) swaps the body's `webhookId` for the registered `alchemyWebhookId_1`, then (5) resolves `{{...}} variables` and HMAC-SHA256-signs the exact final bytes into `X-Alchemy-Signature` — signing the unresolved template would never verify.

**webhookId matters**: the ingest route looks the HMAC signing key up by the payload's `webhookId` in `alchemy_webhook_registry` — DB only, no env fallback — so an unknown id is 404 NOT_CONFIGURED even with a perfect signature. Two working paths:
1. (preferred) Run `_Setup / 4. Bootstrap Alchemy webhooks` — its test script saves `alchemyWebhookId_1` + `alchemySigningKey_1`, and this pre-request substitutes/signs with them automatically.
2. (no Alchemy account) Register the placeholder id yourself: `POST /admin/alchemy-webhooks/signing-keys` (item `_Setup / 4b`) with `webhookId: "wh_sim_evm"` and a signing key of your choosing, then set that key as `alchemySigningKey` in the environment. The pre-request warns in the console when it falls back to the placeholder.

Gotcha: 200 = acknowledged, not credited — ingest runs in the background. Check `GET /api/v1/invoices/{{invoiceId}}` (running `Internal / Cron tick` advances confirmations) to see the payment land.

request.description:
Simulates a BlockCypher `tx-confirmation` push for a watched Litecoin address — the exact TX-object body BlockCypher POSTs when a subscribed address receives a transaction.

Setup: create an invoice whose Litecoin leg is active first (`invoiceReceiveAddress` must hold the LTC receive address — chainId 801), and set `blockcypherIngestToken` to the gateway's `BLOCKCYPHER_INGEST_TOKEN`.

Key fields:
- **URL chainId segment** (`801` = Litecoin; `800` BTC, `802` BTC-testnet3, `803` LTC-testnet) — the URL, not the payload, decides the chain; the payload's coin identity is deliberately ignored.
- **`outputs[].value`** — litoshis (100000000 = 1 LTC). Only outputs paying a watched receive address are credited; the output's vout index becomes `log_index`.
- **`block_height: -1` + `confirmations: 0`** = mempool sighting → `invoice:payment_detected`. BlockCypher re-fires the same tx at each depth: re-send with a positive `block_height` and higher `confirmations` to simulate depth — dedup on `(chain_id, tx_hash, vout)` prevents double-credits, and the confirmation sweeper re-checks depth via RPC anyway.

Gotchas: the route ACKs 200 and processes in the background — 200 never proves a credit landed (check the invoice after an `Internal / Cron tick`). The pre-request script mints a fresh `hash` each send so every run is a NEW payment; comment it out to test idempotent re-delivery instead.

item.name: Admin / Webhook deliveries
item.description:
Operator-only dead-letter queue for outbound webhook deliveries. Use these when a merchant webhook has been failing and you've coordinated a fix.

### Outbound event types (the `eventType` column)

Two scopes, distinct prefixes:
- `invoice:payment_*` — per-transaction events (one fires per contributing on-chain tx)
- `invoice:*` (no `payment_`) — whole-invoice lifecycle events (one fires when the invoice itself transitions)

**Per-transfer audit** (key `{event}:{invoiceId}:{txHash}` — detect→confirm produces TWO distinct rows for the same tx):
- `invoice:payment_detected` — first sighting of a pending contributing tx (push or poll). The tx's `confirmations` will be `0` for push-detected, `n` for poll-detected.
- `invoice:payment_confirmed` — same tx reached the confirmation threshold. Fires for every contributing tx, even on partial-payment invoices that haven't completed yet.

**Whole-invoice lifecycle** (key `{event}:{invoiceId}:{status}`):
- `invoice:completed` — invoice transitioned to `status='completed'`. Overpaid invoices fire this same event with `data.invoice.extraStatus='overpaid'` on the snapshot.
- `invoice:expired` — past `expiresAt` with no completion.
- `invoice:canceled` — merchant aborted via admin route.
- `invoice:demoted` — reorg un-confirmation; key includes `{previousStatus}:{newStatus}`. Carries `previousStatus` and `poolReacquired` / `poolCollided` extras.

**Payout lifecycle**: `payout.submitted`, `payout.confirmed`, `payout.failed`

Payload shape (every invoice event): `{ event, timestamp, data: { invoice, transactions: [{ hash, chainId, chainName, token, isNative, amount, amountRaw, amountUsd, confirmations, status }, ...], triggerTxHash } }`. `transactions[]` lists every contributing tx; `triggerTxHash` points at the tx that caused this event (null for status-only events).

URL/secret resolution: per-resource override → merchant default → silently skipped (with a `warn` log so misconfig is visible). Body is HMAC-SHA256 signed in `X-Webhook-Signature`; merchants should de-dup on `X-Webhook-Idempotency-Key`.

query.description: pending | delivered | dead

request.description: Returns a page of webhook delivery rows filtered by status. Default `status=dead` surfaces rows that exhausted the retry schedule (5 outer attempts × up to 4 inline HTTP attempts each) and require operator intervention before the merchant sees the event.

Resets a row from `dead` to `pending` with `next_attempt_at = now`, so the next scheduled-jobs sweep re-dispatches it. Returns 404 with code `NOT_DEAD` if the row is not in dead status — pending rows are already queued and delivered rows don't need replay.

Set `deadDeliveryId` from the List-dead-deliveries test script, or paste a specific id.

item.name: Admin / Orphans & Audit
item.description: Reconciliation surface for transfers that couldn't be auto-attributed. Orphans are on-chain transfers on addresses with no active invoice and no active cooldown — ingest writes them with `invoice_id=NULL`, `status='orphaned'`, and NO merchant-facing webhooks fire. Operators either attribute them to a specific invoice (normal or terminal — the `viaAdminOverride` path lets expired/canceled invoices flip to `confirmed`/`overpaid` when the credit clears the bar) or dismiss them with a reason. Audit-address closes the loop for 'merchant says they never got X': it re-scans on-chain history for an address and ingests anything missing.

query.description: Optional: narrow to one chain

request.description: Returns open orphans (invoice_id IS NULL AND dismissed_at IS NULL), ordered by `detectedAt DESC`. Backed by a partial index so the queue stays cheap regardless of total tx volume.

Re-points the orphan's `invoice_id` at `invoiceId`, promotes the tx from `orphaned` to `detected`/`confirmed` based on its confirmation count, and runs `recomputeInvoiceFromTransactions` with `viaAdminOverride=true`.

The override lets an `expired` / `canceled` invoice flip to `confirmed` (or `overpaid` on USD-path) when the attributed credit clears the confirm bar. Partial credits against a terminal invoice stay parked — the merchant's decision stands if the payment doesn't cover the invoice.

Returns 404 if the orphan/invoice is unknown, 409 if the tx is already attributed or dismissed.

Stamps `dismissed_at` + `dismiss_reason`. The tx row stays in the `transactions` table for audit, but the partial index drops it from the open-orphans queue.

409 if the tx is attributed to an invoice (not an orphan) or already dismissed.

Re-runs the chain adapter's `scanIncoming` for every registered token on `chainId` (plus the native symbol), diffs against `transactions`, and ingests missing rows through the normal ingest path (so orphan / cooldown / invoice-match rules apply identically).

Use when a merchant reports a missing payment. Idempotent — already-stored txs are silently skipped via the UNIQUE `(chain_id, tx_hash, log_index)` constraint.

`sinceMs` defaults to `now - 30 days` if omitted; the adapter's own max-scan window further clamps the range.

item.name: Admin / Balances
item.description:
Operator-only treasury view. Returns per-address + per-chain + per-family token balances with USD totals, shaped token → address → chain → family → grand total. The top-level `source` field tells you which math produced the numbers.

**Mode `source="db"` (default, fast)**
- Spendable per (chainId, address) = SUM(confirmed `transactions.amountRaw` inbound) − SUM(confirmed `payouts.amountRaw` outbound from this address) − SUM(active `payout_reservations.amountRaw` for this address), clamped at 0.
- No RPC traffic; ~50ms regardless of pool size.
- Addresses with no recorded activity are omitted.
- Caveat: off-system movements are invisible (rare on chains with Alchemy webhooks). Use `?live=true` for reconciliation.

**Mode `source="rpc"` (opts-in via `?live=true`)**
- Fans out one `getAccountBalances` call per (address, chainId) pair across every wired adapter. Authoritative but 15–30s cold.
- Use for post-topup audit. Not for dashboard auto-refresh.

**Caching**: keyed by mode + query string. DB mode TTL = 5s; RPC mode TTL = 60s. `db` and `rpc` entries do not share a slot. Each response carries `cached: true|false`.

request.description: Default mode: balances derived from the `transactions` + `payouts` tables — no RPC traffic. ~50ms. Response `snapshot.source === "db"`. Addresses with no recorded activity are omitted. Use this for dashboards.

query.description: Forces a live on-chain snapshot. Expensive — 15–30s typical for a full mainnet pool.

request.description: Opts into the authoritative RPC path: fans out one `getAccountBalances` call per (address, chainId) pair to every wired chain adapter. Response `snapshot.source === "rpc"`. Use for post-topup reconciliation or audit — never bind to dashboard auto-refresh. Cached 60s.

query.description: evm | tron | solana | utxo

request.description: Restricts the sweep to one family (`evm`, `tron`, `solana`, or `utxo`). Cheaper than the full snapshot when you only want one family's treasury. Cached separately from the unfiltered call.
Single-chain snapshot. Use when investigating a specific chain (e.g. after funding an HD pool address on it).

query.description: Only `pool` exists today; the legacy `fee` value is silently coerced.

request.description: Every HD-derived pool address with its ledger-derived spendable balances. The payout picker reads exactly this data when planning — what you see here matches what the picker will pick.

query.description: Any pool (or unspent-UTXO) address known to the gateway — fee-wallet addresses are NOT enumerated by the snapshot and come back empty. Uses the receive address from the most recent invoice by default.

request.description: Skips the rest of the universe and only snapshots one address. Cheapest possible call — handy when an operator wants to verify a specific wallet without paying the full sweep cost.

item.name: Reconcile ledger to chain — dry-run preview (default)

request.description:
Ledger-vs-chain reconciliation preview. Reads each account-model `address_pool` address's LIVE on-chain balance and reports, per token, `deltaRaw = onChainRaw - ledgerRaw` where the ledger is the SETTLED value (confirmed inbound + intra-pool credits - confirmed outbound + prior adjustments; no in-flight reservations, no zero-clamp).

`dryRun` defaults to true, so this call (and even an empty `{}` or missing body) writes nothing — `adjusted` is always 0. Optional narrowing keys: `family` (evm | tron | solana; utxo/monero are accepted but out of scope → checked: 0), `chainId`, `address` (exact pool address). Unknown keys are rejected (400 VALIDATION — the schema is strict).

Per-address RPC failures land in `errors[]` without aborting the sweep; non-empty `errors` means the result is partial. Run with consolidation schedules paused so balances are quiescent.

exec: console.log('checked:', body.checked, '| drift deltas:', body.deltas.length, '| errors:', body.errors.length);

item.name: Reconcile ledger to chain — apply adjustments (dryRun:false)

request.description:
Applies the reconciliation: for every non-zero delta, writes ONE signed `balance_adjustments` row (reason `rpc-reconciliation`) equal to on-chain minus settled-ledger, so the ledger reads exactly the chain afterwards.

Safe by construction: append-only (never edits transaction/payout history) and idempotent (a second run sees delta 0 and writes nothing). Run the dry-run preview first and PAUSE consolidation schedules while applying, so in-flight movements don't race the snapshot.

Narrow the blast radius with the same optional keys as the preview: `family`, `chainId`, `address` — e.g. `{ "dryRun": false, "family": "evm", "chainId": 137 }`.

item.name: Admin / UTXO RBF (bump fee)
item.description: Replace-By-Fee for stuck UTXO payouts. Bumps a payout's fee on demand when its tx is sitting in the mempool past the merchant's patience. BIP125-compliant: shares inputs with the prior attempt, has strictly higher absolute fee, exceeds the relay's incremental-fee threshold. Strategy cascade: shrink_change → drop_change → add_inputs.

request.description: Re-queries the chain adapter's high-tier feerate and rebroadcasts at that rate (clamped up by `incremental_relay_fee` if the prior attempt was already paying close to it).
Use this when the operator has independent feerate intelligence (mempool.space dashboard, etc.) and wants an exact target rather than the adapter's tier estimate.
Computes the strategy + new fee + change without broadcasting. Useful to confirm whether a bump is feasible (insufficient funds, no fee headroom, etc.) before committing.

item.name: Admin / Migrate
item.description: Operator-only schema migration trigger. Most production runtimes (Workers, Vercel Edge) have no filesystem at runtime and return 501 here — use `wrangler d1 migrations apply` or the Turso CLI for those. Local Node + remote libSQL/Postgres deployments can call this directly.

request.description:
Runs the bundled `drizzle/migrations` set through Drizzle's idempotent migrator — it reads `meta/_journal.json` and applies only migrations not yet recorded in `__drizzle_migrations`. Returns `{ ok: true, folder }` on success, where `folder` is the migrations path that was applied. Node/Deno already run this at boot; this endpoint is for the ops panel's reapply button or after hot-patching the migrations directory.

On runtimes without filesystem access (Workers / Vercel Edge) returns 501 with code `MIGRATIONS_NOT_BUNDLED` — apply migrations CLI-side with `npx drizzle-kit push` pointed at `TURSO_URL` / `TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN` instead.

item.name: Admin / Payout recovery
item.description:
Operator-only escape hatch for payout legs stuck in the HELD state: the main-tx broadcast returned an ambiguous outcome (timeout, dropped connection, gateway restart mid-send), so the gateway can't know whether the transfer actually landed and refuses to guess — re-broadcasting could double-pay, writing it off could strand real funds.

The auto-reconciler (`reconcileUnknownBroadcastPayouts`, part of every cron tick) clears most holds on its own: an exact chain-scan match drives the leg to `submitted`, a confirmed miss releases it for re-broadcast. Anything still listed here after a few ticks needs a human.

Flow:
1. `List held payouts` — surfaces the queue oldest-first and captures the first leg's id into `heldPayoutId`.
2. Find the leg's REAL on-chain transfer on a block explorer using the row's `sourceAddress` / `destinationAddress` / `token` / `amountRaw`. Paste the confirmed hash into `recoveredTxHash` — this value is operator-supplied and must be explorer-verified; the gateway re-verifies (mined, not reverted, exact transfer match, hash not reused) but only a correct hash resolves the hold.
3. `Recover held payout` — drives the leg to `submitted`; the normal confirm sweep finishes the job and clears the phantom-balance over-count.

All requests require `adminKey`.

query.description: Optional: narrow to one chain (integer)
Optional: narrow to one token symbol, e.g. USDT
1..500 (values above 500 are clamped). Default 100.

request.description:
Recovery queue: `standard` / `consolidation_sweep` payout legs whose main-tx broadcast errored ambiguously — status `reserved`/`topping-up`, `txHash` NULL, `broadcastAttemptedAt` set, `lastError` containing "Broadcast outcome unknown". These are exactly the rows `POST /admin/payouts/{id}/recover` acts on.

The auto-reconciler clears most held legs itself (exact chain-scan match drives the leg to `submitted`, or the hold is released for re-broadcast, at which point the leg drops out of this list). Anything still listed needs an operator: use each entry's `sourceAddress`/`destinationAddress`/`token`/`amountRaw` to find the real OUT tx on a block explorer, then feed its hash to the recover request.

Ordered oldest-held first; `heldForMs` is the age since the ambiguous broadcast attempt. Internal `gas_top_up`/`gas_burn` children never appear here.

Escape hatch for a held leg the operator has confirmed on a block explorer DID land on-chain. `txHash` must be the real, successful transfer hash (`0x` + 64 hex chars; body is strict — no extra keys).

The gateway verifies before changing anything:
1. tx mined and NOT reverted — 422 `RECOVERY_TX_NOT_MINED` / `RECOVERY_TX_REVERTED`
2. tx is THIS leg's exact source→destination transfer of token/amount — 422 `RECOVERY_TX_MISMATCH` (if the hash is right, the transfer may be older than the scan window)
3. hash not already attached to another payout — 409 `RECOVERY_TX_ALREADY_USED`

Non-held rows (wrong kind/status, txHash already set, no source) are refused with 409 `PAYOUT_NOT_RECOVERABLE`; unknown id is 404 `PAYOUT_NOT_FOUND`.

On success the row is driven to `submitted` with the verified hash; the normal confirmPayouts sweep then confirms it, posting the source debit + destination credit that clears the phantom-balance over-count.

Set `heldPayoutId` via the List-held-payouts test script (or paste one), and put the explorer hash in `recoveredTxHash`.

exec: pm.test('payout driven to submitted with the txHash attached', () => {

item.name: Admin / UTXO maintenance
item.description:
One-shot data-repair surface for the UTXO chains (BTC/LTC + testnets). Currently a single request: rewrite the `address_index` values corrupted by the pre-migration-0006 multi-family bug (universal invoices stamped UTXO rows with the primary family's counter, breaking payout signing — the signer derives keys from the stored index).

Run it once after deploying migration 0006 on a database that had multi-family UTXO payments. Idempotent and safe to re-run: a healthy database reports zero fixes; `unmatchedAddresses` in the report usually means `MASTER_SEED` was rotated since the rows were minted (unrecoverable here — those rows are left untouched).

Requires `adminKey`.

request.description:
One-shot repair for the multi-family UTXO `address_index` bug fixed in migration 0006. Pre-fix ingest stamped `utxos.address_index` (and the 0006 backfill stamped `invoice_receive_addresses.address_index`) with the invoice's legacy primary-family index — for universal invoices where UTXO was non-primary that was the EVM/Tron/Solana counter value, breaking payout signing (the signer derives the key from the stored index).

Per registered UTXO chain: derives every counter slot from `MASTER_SEED`, builds an address-to-index map, and rewrites any `invoice_receive_addresses` / `utxos` row whose stored index doesn't match. No request body; idempotent — re-running after success is a no-op. Run ONCE after deploying migration 0006 on a database that had pre-fix multi-family UTXO payments.

`unmatchedAddresses` in the report = rows whose address matches no derived slot, usually a rotated `MASTER_SEED` since minting — unrecoverable here (the original seed would be needed); those rows are left untouched.

exec: console.log('invoiceRx fixed:', body.totalInvoiceRxFixed, '| utxos fixed:', body.totalUtxosFixed);

item.name: Admin / Debug (Monero)
item.description:
View-key forensics for "where is my Monero payment?". Monero detection is receiver-side cryptography — the cron scans every tx output against the gateway's subaddresses with the private view key — so a missing payment is always one of two failures: the address wasn't in the scan set, or the crypto didn't match.

- `Scan state` answers the first: which subaddresses are live-for-polling (the cron only scans pending/processing/completed, unexpired invoices), plus scanner height lag vs the daemon tip.
- `Inspect tx` answers the second: a read-only per-output match report (pubkeys tested, subaddress matched, decoded RingCT amount). Nothing is written.
- `Force-ingest tx` credits a confirmed match the cron's live-invoice filter skipped — the late-payment recovery path. Can flip an EXPIRED invoice; make sure that's intended.

Fill `moneroTxHash` by hand (64 hex chars — from the payer or a block explorer); inspect/force-ingest both consume it. Inbound-only surface: Monero is inbound-only in v1, so nothing here (or anywhere) sends XMR out. Inspect reports include derived shared secrets — treat responses as sensitive.

All requests require `adminKey` and 404 with MONERO_NOT_WIRED when no Monero adapter is configured.

item.name: Inspect tx — per-output crypto match report

request.description:
Read-only forensics: fetches ONE tx by hash through the same daemon-RPC failover stack the cron uses and replays the full view-key detection crypto against it.

For every output the report shows: the candidate tx pubkeys tested (primary tag-0x01 + per-output additional tag-0x04), which subaddress matched (if any), the derived shared secret + expected one-time output pubkey, and the decoded RingCT amount in piconero (1 XMR = 1e12).

Body fields:
- `txHash` (required) — 64 hex chars, case-insensitive.
- `addresses` (optional string[]) — override the match set with arbitrary base58 primaries/subaddresses. Default is EVERY family='monero' row in invoice_receive_addresses, INCLUDING expired invoices' subaddresses (forensics, not the hot path).

There is no txKey field — this is receiver-side view-key scanning; no sender secret is needed.

Gotchas: RPC failure and tx-not-found still return HTTP 200 with `ok:false` and a `step` marker — check `ok`, not just the status code. 400 BAD_TX_HASH for malformed hashes; 404 MONERO_NOT_WIRED when no Monero adapter is configured. The report includes derived shared secrets — treat responses as sensitive. Nothing is written; use force-ingest-tx to actually credit a matched payment.

exec: console.warn('inspection failed at step ' + body.step + ': ' + body.error);

console.log('outputs: ' + body.tx.outputCount + ', matched: ' + body.summary.matchedOutputs.length + ' (network ' + body.gateway.network + ', ' + body.gateway.liveSubaddressesChecked + ' subaddresses checked)');

item.name: Scan state — cron visibility + height lag

request.description:
One-call answer to "why isn't my Monero payment being detected?".

The cron only scans subaddresses whose invoice is live (pending/processing/completed) AND unexpired. Everything else silently drops out of the scan set — payments to those addresses are invisible to automatic detection. Every family='monero' address row is returned with `isLiveForPolling` plus a human-readable `droppedReason` naming the failed check (status vs expiry).

`cron` block: liveAddressCount / totalAddressCount / droppedAddressCount, `lastScannedHeight` (the scanner's cached cursor; null if it never completed a tick), `currentTipHeight` (live daemon read, best-effort; null when all RPC backends fail), and `blocksBehindTip` — a growing lag means the scanner is stalled or rate-limited.

If a payment went to a dropped address, recover it with the force-ingest-tx request in this folder.

Errors: 404 MONERO_NOT_WIRED when no Monero adapter is configured.

exec: console.warn(dropped.length + ' monero subaddress(es) are NOT scanned by the cron — payments to them need force-ingest-tx');
console.warn('scanner is ' + body.cron.blocksBehindTip + ' blocks behind tip — check cron health / RPC rate limits');

request.description:
Late-payment recovery: runs the same crypto matching as the cron against one tx and INGESTS every matched output — bypassing the cron's live-invoice filter entirely. Use after inspect-tx confirms a match.

Match set: ALL family='monero' rows in invoice_receive_addresses (no expiry/status filter). Each matched output with a positive decoded RingCT amount goes through the standard ingestDetectedTransfer: a `transactions` row (token XMR, logIndex = output index, sender recorded as `monero_unknown_sender`) attributed to the owning invoice — or an orphan row when none exists. Invoice status recomputes through the normal path and merchant webhooks fire as configured. An EXPIRED invoice can flip as a result — make sure that's intended.

Idempotent: transactions are unique on (chainId, txHash, logIndex); re-runs land in `summary.duplicates` rather than double-crediting.

Mempool txs are accepted (blockHeight null → recorded at block 0 until mined).

Errors: 400 BAD_JSON / BAD_TX_HASH; 404 MONERO_NOT_WIRED (adapter not configured) or TX_NOT_FOUND (daemon doesn't know the hash); 502 RPC_FAILED (all daemon backends failed).

exec: console.log('matched: ' + body.summary.outputsMatched + ', ingested: ' + body.summary.ingested + ', duplicates: ' + body.summary.duplicates + ', errors: ' + body.summary.errors);

item.name: Internal
item.description: Endpoints the gateway operator hits directly — not the merchant surface, not public.

request.description:
Runs the full scheduled-jobs sweep out of band — the exact sequence the Workers `scheduled` handler runs, in order:

`warmRateCache → pollPayments → confirmTransactions → recheckConfirmedForReorg → executeReservedPayouts → reconcileUnknownBroadcastPayouts → confirmPayouts → reconcileFailedPayoutGasBurns → reconcileConfirmedPayoutGasBurns → sweepStuckPayoutReservations → sweepExpiredInvoices → reconcileOrphanedAllocations → reconcileOrphanedMoneroAllocations → sweepWebhookDeliveries → runAutoConsolidations` — plus `alchemySyncAddresses` (only when Alchemy Notify is configured) and `blockcypherSyncSubscriptions` (only when BlockCypher is configured; drains the per-address subscribe/unsubscribe queue).

Outside Cloudflare Workers / Deno Deploy (native cron), this is the only way to advance state between invoices — Vercel Cron hits it on a schedule; for local Node runs invoke it manually after creating an invoice and simulating a webhook.

Returns `{ ok: true, result: { <job>: { ok, value | error } } }`. **Per-job failures still return HTTP 200** — check each job's `ok` flag (the test script warns on any `ok: false`). No request body. 401 = wrong `cronSecret`; 404 = `CRON_SECRET` not set on the gateway.

exec: const failed = Object.entries(body.result || {}).filter(([, o]) => o && o.ok === false);
if (failed.length > 0) { console.warn('jobs failed this tick:', failed.map(([name, o]) => name + ': ' + o.error).join(' | ')); }
